commands.review_summary:
The PR switches MiniMax global/CN OAuth code and token requests from `api.* /oauth/*` to `account.* /oauth2/*`, adds endpoint-selection regression coverage, and adds a changelog entry.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +61, Docs +1. Total +64 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main posts MiniMax OAuth forms to `api.* /oauth/*`, live MiniMax probes show those endpoints redirect to `account.* /oauth2/*`, and the fetch guard drops unsafe cross-origin redirect bodies by default. I did not run the full OpenClaw login flow locally in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This is not a repair-lane candidate because the main blocker is contributor real behavior proof for their setup, not a concrete code change ClawSweeper can safely supply.

target.title: fix(minimax): use account OAuth device endpoints
target.body:
## Summary
- update MiniMax OAuth to call the current account device-code endpoints directly
- keep the provider API base URLs unchanged for post-login resource/config handling
- add a regression test for both global and CN OAuth endpoint selection

## Why
MiniMax now redirects `api.minimax.io/oauth/code` and `api.minimaxi.com/oauth/code` to `account.*` device-code endpoints. OpenClaw's guarded fetch intentionally strips POST bodies on cross-origin redirects, so the redirected MiniMax request receives an empty form and fails with `client_id is required`.

## Validation
- `pnpm test extensions/minimax/oauth.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/minimax/oauth.ts extensions/minimax/oauth.test.ts CHANGELOG.md`
- `git diff --check`

Note: `pnpm changed:lanes --json` is not useful in this fork checkout because local `origin/main` is divergent from `upstream/main` and reports thousands of unrelated upstream differences. The PR branch diff against `upstream/main` is limited to `CHANGELOG.md`, `extensions/minimax/oauth.ts`, and `extensions/minimax/oauth.test.ts`.
target.author: matthewschleder
